description: Extensible Markup Language document. XML is a text-based markup language that defines rules for encoding documents in a format readable by both humans and machines. Files begin with the XML declaration <?xml followed by version and encoding attributes.
author: WPFHexaEditor Team

functions.parseXMLDeclaration: Parse the <?xml ... ?> processing instruction to extract version and encoding
functions.extractUTF8String: Extract text content from an XML document as UTF-8
functions.detectBOM: Detect byte order mark (EF BB BF for UTF-8, FF FE for UTF-16 LE, FE FF for UTF-16 BE) preceding the XML declaration

blocks.name: XML Declaration Start
blocks.description: The opening <?xml sequence that identifies this file as an XML document (hex 3C 3F 78 6D 6C)

blocks.name: Space After Declaration
blocks.description: Mandatory whitespace character (0x20) separating <?xml from the version attribute

blocks.name: Version and Encoding Region
blocks.description: Region containing version and encoding attributes, e.g. version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>. Actual length varies per file; this captures the typical declaration.

Software: Text editors (VS Code, Notepad++, Sublime Text)
All major programming languages and frameworks

TechnicalDetails.DeclarationRequired: Recommended but technically optional in XML 1.0
